title: #39 ¿Que pasó? with Señor B - week of 12/22
description: Last newsletter of the year :D (Shirt giveaway at the end)
author: Que paso? with Senor B

title: #38 ¿Que pasó? with Señor B - week of 12/15
description: This newsletter exposes the reality of human rights abuses in Florida’s detention centers, provides a vital emergency tool to combat the rise of racial profiling and ICE raids, and debunks the dangerous myths surrounding the detention of U.S. citizens and non-criminals.
author: Que paso? with Senor B

title: #37 ¿Que pasó? with Señor B - week of 12/8
description: This week, we expose the shameful lie about the deportation of veteran Sae Joon Park, sound the alarm on the threat to our community safe havens, and fact-check the dangerous misinformation designed to suppress our vote.
author: Que paso? with Senor B

title: #36 ¿Que pasó? with Señor B - week of 12/1
description: We tackle the week's biggest political denials and hypocrisies, from debunking the idea that ICE's staff diversity shields it from racism and exposing a cruel anti-immigrant restaurant, to personally confronting the affordability crisis and discussing the path forward with Senator Adam Schiff's Housing BOOM Act.
author: Que paso? with Senor B

title: #35 ¿Que pasó? with Señor B - week of 11/24
description: This week, we expose the DHS's despicable, fact-free campaign to blame immigrants (using the far-right dog whistle of "Remigration") for economic woes, while sharing my skeptical-but-engaged conversation with Congressman Eric Swalwell about his stance on our communities.
author: Que paso? with Senor B

title: #34 ¿Que pasó? with Señor B - week of 11/17
description: This week's newsletter analyzes four critical events: a judge ruling the National Guard's Portland deployment illegal; the viral incident of an official pouring bleach on a Denver street vendor's food; a U.S. citizen being detained by ICE in a worksite raid; and a deep-dive into the Mexico protests amidst allegations of foreign funding and manufactured consent.
author: Que paso? with Senor B

title: #33 ¿Que pasó? with Señor B - week of 11/10
description: This week's newsletter exposes the confirmed strategy of an ICE official to use fear and high arrest numbers against non-criminals in our community, while simultaneously highlighting the political hypocrisy of the Justice Department suing California's voter-approved redistricting map and the administration's frantic attempts to suppress the release of the revealing Epstein files.
author: Que paso? with Senor B

title: #32 ¿Que pasó? with Señor B - week of 11/3
description: Facing both the fatal real-world consequences of anti-immigrant propaganda and the rise of deceptive AI videos, this week's issue analyzes the need for community vigilance, while the passage of Prop 50 serves as a reminder to hold elected Democrats accountable to their promises of tangible support for vulnerable communities.
author: Que paso? with Senor B

title: #31 ¿Que pasó? with Señor B - week of 10/27
description: This week, we expose the fear-mongering lies blaming immigrants for the government shutdown, define the dangerous far-right concept of "Remigration" and its link to current deportation threats, and celebrate the crucial win of a father illegally detained by ICE being released to reunite with his daughter battling cancer.
author: Que paso? with Senor B

title: #30 ¿Que pasó? with Señor B - week of 10/20
description: This week, we expose the reality of migrants afraid to call the police, demand accountability from the San Jose Sharks for an offensive jumbotron message, and compare the drastically opposed National Guard missions under Trump (intimidation) and Newsom (humanitarian aid).
author: Que paso? with Senor B

title: #29 ¿Que pasó? with Señor B - week of 10/13
description: The newsletter features a report on the massive nationwide "No Kings in America" protests demanding democratic rights, condemns the President's actions to push a Columbus Day narrative over Indigenous People's Day, and criticizes the administration's dangerous decision to revoke visas over non-citizens' critical social media posts about Charlie Kirk's death, exposing political hypocrisy on free speech.
author: Que paso? with Senor B

title: #28 ¿Que pasó? with Señor B - week of 10/6
description: This week, we expose the President's lies about Portland and the abuse of la migra, while activating California voters to to protect democracy from political rigging.
author: Que paso? with Senor B

title: #27 ¿Que pasó? with Señor B - week of 9/29
description: We detail a week of massive government overreach: from ICE agents harming teens and U.S. citizens in militarized Chicago raids and the President's unhinged AI videos, to political scapegoating during the shutdown and alarming attempts to dictate conservative policy to U.S. universities.
author: Que paso? with Senor B

title: #25 ¿Que pasó? with Señor B - week of 9/15
description: From deported mothers and detained students to violent arrests of legal residents, this newsletter shares the human stories behind the headlines while highlighting the inspiring acts of resistance from our community.
author: Que paso? with Senor B

title: #24 ¿Que pasó? with Señor B - week of 9/8
description: This newsletter goes behind the headlines to expose the hypocrisy and injustice in stories of deportation, cultural appropriation, and political rhetoric that impact marginalized communities
author: Que paso? with Senor B

title: #23 ¿Que pasó? with Señor B - week of 9/1
description: A critique of Kristi Noem's false claims about Los Angeles, a new California law that would require schools to notify families about ICE presence, a look at the controversial plan to use military judges for immigration cases, and finally, a summary of Illinois Governor Pritzker's warnings about federal intervention in Chicago.
author: Que paso? with Senor B

title: #22 ¿Que pasó? with Señor B - week of 8/25
description: The harsh detention of a woman in a sanctuary city, an auto body shop worker spewing racist remarks, a man facing an abusive deportation choice, a video revealing unsanitary conditions in an ICE facility, and a man being detained by ICE moments after being granted asylum.
author: Que paso? with Senor B

title: #21 ¿Que pasó? with Señor B - week of 8/18
description: Law enforcement tactics to discredit protestors, the removal of protections for families from ICE raids, the questionable actions of alleged ICE agents, the shutdown of a detention center, and the dangerous trend of influencers pranking immigrant communities by impersonating ICE agents.
author: Que paso? with Senor B

title: #20 ¿Que pasó? with Señor B - week of 8/11
description: A protest rebranded as a music festival and an ICE agent's chilling confession to a former detention center employee's testimony, ICE agents appearing at a political rally, and an influencer's harmful narrative about migrants and citizenship.
author: Que paso? with Senor B

title: #19 ¿Que pasó? with Señor B - week of 8/4
description: A hunger strike by migrants at "Alligator Alcatraz" protesting inhumane conditions, a police officer in Los Angeles threatening boys and the importance of recording such interactions, and the cultural appropriation lawsuit against Adidas and designer Willy Chavarria for their huarache-inspired shoe design.
author: Que paso? with Senor B

title: #18 ¿Que pasó? with Señor B - week of 7/28
description: ICE's student loan forgiveness, a father's deportation in New York, the White House's use of memes, and a call for support for DACA recipients.
author: Que paso? with Senor B

title: #17 ¿Que pasó? with Señor B - week of 7/21
description: horrifying realities of ICE agents joking about detaining people and receiving bonuses, the abandonment of a pregnant woman by immigration officers after her husband's forced removal, the injustice in the William McNeal Jr. case despite initial compliance issues, and the current administration's subtle yet dangerous promotion of white supremacist ideals, highlighting the hypocrisy in how "settlers" and "immigrants" are perceived.
author: Que paso? with Senor B

title: #16 ¿Que pasó? with Señor B - week of 7/14
description: The alarming realities of online privacy threats and the deeply disturbing, often inhumane, conditions within immigrant detention facilities, highlighting cases of neglect, the erosion of due process, and the confirmed profiling by ICE, all while emphasizing the urgent need for humanity and accountability.
author: Que paso? with Senor B

title: #15 ¿Que pasó? with Señor B - week of 7/7
description: Inhumane detention of a U.S. citizen veteran by immigration authorities, the impending loss of protections for hundreds of thousands of TPS holders, the shocking conditions within a new Florida detention facility, police complicity in migrant disappearances, and a recent devastating ICE raid on California farmworkers.
author: Que paso? with Senor B

title: #14 ¿Que pasó? with Señor B - week of 6/30
description: The ongoing incarceration of Alex Enamorado, the closure of a rural Nebraska hospital due to budget cuts despite local voting patterns, ICE's controversial opening of a private migrant facility in California, the President's unsubstantiated claims about educational spending on translation services, and the importance of confronting hateful rhetoric.
author: Que paso? with Senor B

title: #13 ¿Que pasó? with Señor B - week of 6/23
description: Ontario Mayor's insensitive comments on immigration, exposes deceptive new ICE tactics at hospitals and detention centers, and reminds everyone, including migrants, of their constitutional rights
author: Que paso? with Senor B

title: #12 ¿Que pasó? with Señor B - week of 6/16
description: Colorado cop sharing private student info leading to potential deportation, an ICE agent pointing a gun at a citizen documenting an obscured license plate, ICE detaining a U.S. citizen at Walmart with false accusations, and ICE agents caught changing vehicle plates to avoid accountability
author: Que paso? with Senor B

title: #11 ¿Que pasó? with Señor B - week of 6/9
description: complexities of protests, differentiating between genuine demonstrators and looters, question the alarming trend of legally documented individuals being detained by ICE, and advocate for respectful language by urging against the use of the term "illegal" for migrants.
author: Que paso? with Senor B

title: #10 ¿Que pasó? with Señor B - week of 6/2
description: This week's newsletter exposes the calculated cruelty of an immigration system that profits from caging families, blocks lawmakers from investigating its abuses, and unjustly targets its own victims for deportation.
author: Que paso? with Senor B

title: #8 ¿Que pasó? with Señor B - week of 5/19
description: Hope from a student's release and a graduate who inspired millions meets urgent alerts on ICE courthouse tactics
author: Que paso? with Senor B

title: #7 ¿Que pasó? with Señor B - week of 5/12
description: California bill ensuring legal counsel for unaccompanied immigrant minors, scams targeting migrants, scrutiny over the Trump administration's refugee policies for White South Africans, and a warning about fabricated online content exploiting community trauma
author: Que paso? with Senor B

title: #6 ¿Que pasó? with Señor B - week of 5/5
description: the human cost of immigration policies through Ximena's detention, celebrating teachers while advocating for their increased support and examines the controversy surrounding a viral racist incident and the contrasting financial support it generated
author: Que paso? with Senor B

title: #2 ¿Que pasó? with Señor B - week of 4/7
description: Controversies Erupt Over Migrant Treatment, School Access, and Deportation Policies
author: Que paso? with Senor B

title: #1 ¿Que pasó? with Señor B - week of 4/1
description: Unpacking crucial issues: Trump's third term, migrant rights (detention, asylum, education), and practical preparedness tips. Join our growing community for news and action.
author: Que paso? with Senor B
